comment.body: Risk level refers to the *probability* of large price swings or changes in market direction. For example SPX this morning reversed at 5917ish  (vs. Alma's Risk Level 5920.38 intraday post), this involves evaluating how likely the market is to experience sharp movements, either upward or downward, based on current price patterns, volatility, and market conditions.

comment.body: OFF topic. Further to my mention of URA (uranium miners) the other day i will just draw attention to this chart of ten largest trades in URA in the last year. You can see they have two clusters 1 at the paek last year and now at the lows. The shape of the markets is diamonds for sweeps(urgent) round for blocks... blue is on lit exchange light orange is dark pool. So three of largest 10 trades in last two days. all sweeps in dark pools. I would say its setting in a reasonable bottom here. The number inisde the tarde is the size since inception of the ETF so No1 and 4 and 11 biggest since inception in last two days...
